items.title: Prof. Ombuki-Berman Receives NSERC Discovery Grant Renewal
items.excerpt: BICIL has been awarded a renewed NSERC Discovery Grant supporting continued research in bio-inspired optimization and its applications to complex real-world problems.

items.title: New Paper Accepted at IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
items.excerpt: Our paper on multi-swarm approaches for dynamic multi-objective optimization has been accepted at IEEE TEVC — one of the top venues in evolutionary computation.

items.title: Lab Members to Present at GECCO 2024 in Melbourne
items.excerpt: Two PhD students will present their research on GA-based feature selection and swarm robotics at GECCO 2024 in Melbourne, Australia.

items.title: Welcome to Three New Graduate Researchers
items.excerpt: BICIL welcomes three new graduate students to our MSc and PhD programs, bringing fresh perspectives to our optimization research agenda.

items.title: New Industry Partnership for Supply Chain Optimization
items.excerpt: We are excited to announce a research collaboration with a regional logistics partner to apply evolutionary algorithms to real supply chain optimization challenges.

items.title: BICIL Hosts Two-Day Metaheuristics Workshop
items.excerpt: The lab hosted an Ontario-wide workshop on metaheuristic algorithms, bringing together researchers from six universities to share advances in bio-inspired computing.
